So apparently when Yellowstone decides to throw the world's most destructive tantrum, the first casualty is America's breadbasket getting absolutely nuked by volcanic ash. Because nothing says 'agricultural disaster' like Mother Nature crop-dusting seven states with literal death powder.

Oh look, food prices only went up 400% overnight because apparently the global economy runs on American grain like some kind of carbohydrate-powered Ponzi scheme. But sure, let's keep pretending we're not all just three missed harvests away from complete societal collapse.

Fifteen million Americans suddenly decided they don't want to live in Pompeii 2.0 and are now fleeing like extras from a disaster movie. The UN is calling it a 'migration crisis' but honestly, what did they expect people to do, throw ash-themed block parties?

NASA confirms that volcanic ash is apparently nature's giant umbrella, dropping global temperatures by 2°C because the sun is now playing hard to get. Climate scientists are probably somewhere crying into their now-useless global warming models.

Europe's solar panels are about as useful as chocolate teapots right now with an 80% drop in power generation. Turns out renewable energy has one tiny flaw: it actually needs that big glowing orb in the sky to work properly.

The global supply chain collapsed faster than a house of cards in a hurricane because manufacturers apparently need this thing called 'light' to see what they're doing. Revolutionary concept, really, using sunlight for manufacturing in the 21st century.

Are you absolutely kidding me right now? TWENTY-THREE DOLLARS for a burrito because of an avocado shortage? I don't care if Yellowstone turned the entire planet into a volcanic winter hellscape, charging twenty-three goddamn dollars for what is essentially beans and rice wrapped in a tortilla is highway robbery and I will not stand for this capitalist nightmare masquerading as a 'market adjustment' you absolute monsters!
